commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=011703e98fd6a4234b11247cd3b12711e79f9839)
commands.review_summary:
This PR statically imports status formatting and queue helpers, makes remaining status lazy imports retry or degrade, wraps `/status` rendering with a 10-second fallback, promotes LINE HTTP error logging to warnings, and adds status/LINE tests.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +135. Total +156 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for a source-level path: current main awaits `buildStatusText` without a fallback and memoizes lazy import promises, so a rejected status import can propagate before a reply. No live LINE reproduction was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed because the external PR lacks required real behavior proof and has multiple diff-caused lint/type/dependency blockers to address before normal review can continue.

target.title: fix(status): LINE /status intermittent no-response — fallback, timeout, and retry hardening
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes LINE `/status` intermittently returning no response by hardening the dynamic module loaders and adding a safety-net timeout.

### Changes (5 commits, +212/-56 across 8 files)

| Phase | What | Rationale |
|:------|:-----|:---------|
| **Phase 0+1** | `.catch(() => undefined)` fallbacks on all 4 dynamic runtime loaders | Previously, a transient import failure would propagate uncaught — now each loader returns sensible defaults instead of throwing |
| **Phase 2** | Eliminate 2 fragile dynamic-import wrappers (`status-message.runtime.ts`, `status-queue.runtime.ts`) via precise static imports; fix `??=` cached-rejection on remaining 3 loaders by resetting promise to `null` on reject | Static imports eliminate the dynamic-hop failure point; promise reset removes the permanent-cache poison so transient failures are retried |
| **Phase 2 fix** | Replace `?? {} as any` false-positive in plugin health line with proper early-return guard | Previously a loader failure would show "🔌 Plugins: OK" instead of "⚠️ Plugins: health unavailable" |
| **Phase 3** | Upgrade `logVerbose` → `warn` in `logLineHttpError` for production visibility; add 10s `setTimeout` race in `buildStatusReply` | Without the timeout, a hanging render produces zero response to the user; with `logVerbose` the error was invisible in production logs |
| **Tests** | Timeout path test (fake timers + never-settling promise); `??=` retry pattern test (fail → retry → succeed) | Critical regression guards for the two core fix mechanisms |

### Key Fix Details

**The "no response" root cause** — `buildStatusText` makes 4 dynamic imports using the `??=` pattern that caches the promise permanently. If any import transiently fails (network hiccup, module compilation delay), the rejection is cached forever and the status command gets stuck on every subsequent attempt. Phase 2 fixes this by resetting the promise cache on rejection.

**The timeout safety net** — Even with the import fix, a segment of the status rendering could hang indefinitely. The 10s timeout in `buildStatusReply` ensures the user always gets a response with a fallback error message.

### Tests
- `commands-status.test.ts`: 19 tests — covers timeout path (new), throw path, and all existing scenarios
- `status-text.test.ts`: 7 tests — covers `??=` retry pattern (new, fail→retry→succeed), channel features, plugin health
- `send.test.ts`: 14 tests — updated assertions for `warnMock`
- TypeScript: `pnpm tsgo` → 0 errors

### Closes
Closes openclaw/openclaw#94626

---

### Types of Changes
- [x] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to change)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation (non-code change that improves documentation)
- [ ] 🔧 Chore (refactoring, test updates, CI configuration changes)
